question: Are there significant anomalies or spikes in methane emissions for any country-sector combination in a given year?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap effectively visualizes anomalies or spikes across many categorical combinations (Country-Sector vs. Year). By encoding emission deviations (z-score or min-max normalized) by color, major anomalies (spikes) within each country-sector row stand out. This makes it easy to visually scan many combinations and years for significant spikes or outlier years, addressing the analytical focus across the full table. The structure (195 countries x 9 sectors) means a heatmap allows a dense, high-level overview while preserving the ability to inspect granular spikes.
analysis.target_insight: Which country-sector combinations saw major year-over-year emission spikes, pinpointed by visually prominent color shifts on specific years.

chart_encodings.color: Normalized deviation in emission (per country-sector row, per year)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Colorbar for emission anomaly scale; sort rows descending by max deviation; axis tick trimming for large row count

transformations: Unpivot year columns from '1990' to '2018' into a single 'Year' column with emissions as value
Group by ['Country', 'Sector'] to create a panel with emission time-series per combination
For each ['Country', 'Sector'], compute per-year z-score/deviation from its own mean to identify spikes
Reshape (pivot) so that rows are ['Country', 'Sector'], columns are 'Year', and values are normalized deviation
Sort rows by maximum emission deviation to highlight the most anomalous combinations
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\2.csv')
years = [str(y) for y in range(1990, 2019)]
df_melt = df.melt(id_vars=['Country','Sector'], value_vars=years, var_name='Year', value_name='Emissions')
df_melt['Emissions_z'] = df_melt.groupby(['Country','Sector'])['Emissions'].transform(lambda x: (x-x.mean())/x.std(ddof=0))
heatmap_data = df_melt.pivot_table(index=['Country','Sector'], columns='Year', values='Emissions_z')
heatmap_data = heatmap_data.loc[heatmap_data.max(axis=1).sort_values(ascending=False).index]
plt.figure(figsize=(16, 14))
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
im = plt.imshow(heatmap_data.values, aspect='auto', cmap=cmap, vmin=-3, vmax=3)
plt.colorbar(im, label='Emission spike z-score')
plt.xticks(np.arange(len(years)), years, rotation=90)
top_rows = heatmap_data.index[:30]
plt.yticks(np.arange(len(heatmap_data)), [f'{c} ({s})' for c,s in heatmap_data.index])
plt.title('Methane Emissions Anomalies\nCountry-Sector vs. Year (Normalized z-score)')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Country (Sector)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
